metadata.description: Expanded integration tests including newly added datasets and filter tests (2025-2026)
metadata.changes_8.2: Added SCXA single-cell expression tests (CL→scxa_gene_experiment, Gene→scxa_gene_experiment) and CellxGene celltype tests (CL/UBERON/MONDO→cellxgene_celltype). Total: 469 tests.
metadata.changes_8.1: Added advanced_multihop_pipelines category with 11 novel multi-hop queries (4-8 hops): BioGRID interaction to pathways, metabolite to rare disease genes, cell type to BioGRID/drugs/clinical trials, tissue to lipid pathways, phenotype to interaction network/clinical trials, pathway to diseases via ClinVar, Orphanet to ChEMBL/PubChem. Total: 459 tests.
metadata.changes_7.0: Added tests for all 2026-02-04 fixes: BUG-018 (PharmGKB variant→clinical), BUG-020 (ClinVar→dbSNP), BUG-021 (RNACentral text search). Added rare_diseases category (Orphanet, MIM). Added metabolomics category (HMDB). Updated BUG-011/012/016 from known_issues to fixed. Added HPO alternative paths (orphanet, mim, hmdb).
metadata.changes_6.0: Fixed BUG-014/015: MSigDB↔GO and MSigDB↔HPO bidirectional mappings. Fixed HGNC ID resolution (HGNC:#### vs gene symbol). Added MSigDB gene_sets test category.
metadata.changes_5.9: Fixed BUG-013: UniProt↔BindingDB mapping now works. Parser extracts UniProt IDs from numbered chain columns (Chain 1-50).
metadata.changes_5.8: Added HPO disease mapping tests (HPO↔OMIM, HPO↔Orphanet from phenotype.hpoa integration)
metadata.changes_5.7: Fixed filter tests with .0 suffix for floats. Added AlphaFold, Bgee, PubChem Lipinski, GWAS pvalue_mlog, Clinical Trials filters
metadata.changes_5.6: Added comprehensive filter tests for PubChem, ChEMBL, ClinVar, AlphaMissense, PharmGKB, GenCC, GWAS, GO, UniProt, InterPro, Antibody datasets
metadata.changes_5.5: Added MeSH bridge CTD tests, Reactome to Ensembl, CellXGene reverse mappings, clinical trials high-count, MeSH breast cancer hierarchy
metadata.changes_8.0: Added 50 new tests from multi-agent discovery session 2: patent_ip (4), clinical_trials_deep (3), rare_disease_deep (8), cell_type_biology (7), reverse_drug (4), ultra_long_chains (3), interpro_domain (4), gene_set_advanced (8), evidence_triangulation (3), metabolomics (7). New categories: patent_ip, clinical_trials_deep, rare_disease_deep, cell_type_biology, reverse_drug, ultra_long_chains, interpro_domain, gene_set_advanced, evidence_triangulation. Total: 448 tests.

test_categories.gene_mapping: Gene symbol/ID to various resources (proteins, transcripts, GO terms)
test_categories.drug_discovery: Pathway-based and tissue-specific drug target discovery
test_categories.pathways: Pathway and network analysis (Reactome, STRING, Rhea)
test_categories.ontologies: Ontology navigation (GO, EFO, taxonomy, UBERON, CL hierarchies)
test_categories.protein_interactions: Molecular interaction networks (IntAct): protein-protein, protein-small molecule, protein-RNA, protein-gene, mRNA-miRNA
test_categories.gene_expression: Tissue and cell-specific expression (Bgee, UBERON, CL)
test_categories.ncbi_datasets: NCBI/Entrez Gene, RefSeq, PubChem compound mappings
test_categories.data_validation: Data quality validation - attribute correctness, counts, known facts
test_categories.antibody: Therapeutic and structural antibody data (TheraSAbDab, SAbDab, IMGT)
test_categories.clinical_trials: Clinical trial data and disease ontology mappings (ClinicalTrials.gov, MONDO)
test_categories.pubchem_drug: PubChem drug discovery paths (bioactivity, targets, metabolites, CTD)
test_categories.variant_pharmacogenomics: Variant analysis and pharmacogenomics (dbSNP, PharmGKB, AlphaMissense)
test_categories.disease_drug: Disease to drug discovery workflows (MONDO, ChEMBL, DrugCentral, antibodies)
test_categories.expression_celltype: Expression analysis (Bgee, CellXGene, SCXA) and cell type ontology
test_categories.pathway_network: Pathway and network analysis (Reactome hierarchy, STRING, GO reverse)
test_categories.ontology_hierarchy: Ontology hierarchy traversal (MONDO, HPO, EFO parent/child)
test_categories.filters_working: Working filter tests - verified CEL filter expressions
test_categories.filters_broken: Known broken filters - document issues for fixing
test_categories.known_issues: Tests for known bugs - expected to fail until fixed
test_categories.rare_diseases: Rare disease workflows (Orphanet, MIM/OMIM, HPO phenotype-disease mappings)
test_categories.metabolomics: Metabolite analysis (HMDB metabolites, cross-references to PubChem, ChEBI, UniProt)
test_categories.rnacentral_search: RNACentral text search by RNA name, gene symbol, and RNA type
test_categories.patent_ip: Patent and IP analysis pipelines (gene/disease to SureChEMBL patents via PubChem)
test_categories.clinical_trials_deep: Deep clinical trial pipelines (disease to trial drug targets, literature, protein targets)
test_categories.rare_disease_deep: Rare disease deep pipelines (Orphanet/MIM to pathways, interactions, drugs, expression)
test_categories.cell_type_biology: Cell type biology and single-cell (CL ontology, CellXGene reverse queries, tissue drug targets)
test_categories.reverse_drug: Reverse drug-to-biology chains (drug to biological processes, diseases, tissue expression)
test_categories.ultra_long_chains: Ultra-long chains (10+ hops: phenotype to patents, tissue to literature)
test_categories.interpro_domain: InterPro domain analysis (domain to genes, drugs, pathways, structures)
test_categories.gene_set_advanced: Gene set advanced workflows (MSigDB multi-hop, disease to gene sets, GO to drugs)
test_categories.evidence_triangulation: Evidence triangulation and multi-database integration patterns
test_categories.advanced_multihop_pipelines: Advanced multi-hop pipelines (4-8 hops): BioGRID network to pathways, metabolite to rare disease, cell type to drugs/interactions, phenotype to clinical trials, Orphanet to drug candidates

tests.name: Gene Symbol to Uniprot
tests.why: Core mapping: researchers start with gene symbols and need protein IDs for downstream analysis

tests.name: Gene Symbol to Reviewed Uniprot (Swiss-Prot)
tests.why: Get only manually curated Swiss-Prot entries for higher confidence protein data

tests.name: Gene Symbol to Human Reviewed UniProt
tests.why: Human-specific mapping to Swiss-Prot - used in AlphaFold and IntAct paths for precise mapping

tests.name: Gene to GO Biological Process
tests.why: Understand what biological processes a gene is involved in - essential for functional annotation

tests.name: Gene to GO Molecular Function
tests.why: Identify molecular activities (kinase, binding, etc.) - critical for drug target analysis

tests.name: Ensembl Gene to Transcripts
tests.why: Find all transcript isoforms - critical for alternative splicing analysis and RNA-seq

tests.name: Gene to Protein Coding Transcripts
tests.why: Filter only protein-coding isoforms, excluding ncRNAs for proteomics workflows

tests.name: Cancer Genes to Uniprot via HGNC
tests.why: Cancer gene panel analysis - get curated protein entries for tumor suppressor genes

tests.name: Protein to InterPro Domains
tests.why: Map proteins to functional domains - essential for understanding protein architecture and mutations

tests.name: Protein to InterPro Families
tests.why: Identify protein families for evolutionary analysis and functional prediction

tests.name: Gene to Mutation Features
tests.why: Find experimentally verified mutations - critical for variant interpretation and cancer research

tests.name: Gene to Sequence Variants
tests.why: Get known protein variants for clinical variant interpretation and population genetics

tests.name: Protein to PDB Structures
tests.why: Find 3D structures for drug design, protein engineering, and structural biology

tests.name: Protein to NMR Structures
tests.why: Get solution structures (NMR) vs crystal structures - different conformational states

tests.name: Protein to Reactome Pathways
tests.why: Identify biological pathways - critical for systems biology and drug target prioritization

tests.name: Protein to Signaling Pathways
tests.why: Focus on signaling cascades - key for cancer biology and targeted therapy

tests.name: Transcript to Exons
tests.why: Get exon structure for splice variant analysis and exome sequencing

tests.name: Gene to CDS (Coding Sequences)
tests.why: Get coding sequences for codon usage analysis and ORF prediction

tests.name: Gene to Orthologs
tests.why: Find orthologs across species for comparative genomics and model organism studies

tests.name: Human Gene to Mouse Orthologs
tests.why: Map human genes to mouse for translational research and model validation

tests.name: Gene to Paralogs
tests.why: Find gene duplications within species - important for functional redundancy studies

tests.name: Affymetrix Probe to Transcript
tests.why: Map microarray probes to current transcripts - essential for legacy data reanalysis

tests.name: Affymetrix Probe to Gene Symbol
tests.why: Convert probe IDs to gene symbols for publication and meta-analysis - HGNC info is embedded in ensembl attributes

tests.name: Compound to ChEMBL Activities
tests.why: Get bioactivity data for compound - essential for SAR analysis and lead optimization

tests.name: Compound to IC50 Activities
tests.why: Filter for IC50 measurements - standard potency metric for drug discovery

tests.name: Compound to High-Potency Activities
tests.why: Find highly active compounds for hit prioritization

tests.name: ChEMBL Document to Assays
tests.why: Get experimental assays from publications - useful for protocol reproduction

tests.name: ChEMBL Assay to Target Proteins
tests.why: Map assays to protein targets for mechanism of action studies

tests.name: ChEMBL Target to UniProt
tests.why: Link drug targets to protein sequences for structure-based drug design

tests.name: Cancer Genes to ChEMBL Targets
tests.why: Find drugs targeting cancer genes - critical for precision oncology

tests.name: GO Term to Parent Terms
tests.why: Navigate ontology hierarchy upward - useful for enrichment analysis rollup

tests.name: GO Term to Biological Process Parents
tests.why: Filter parent terms by aspect - maintain biological process semantics

tests.name: Taxonomy to Children Taxa
tests.why: Find sub-species or strains - important for microbiome and comparative studies

tests.name: Taxonomy Grand-children (2 levels down)
tests.why: Navigate deeper in taxonomy tree for detailed organism classification

tests.name: Taxonomy to Grand-parent
tests.why: Navigate up taxonomy tree to find broader classifications

tests.name: EFO Disease to Child Terms
tests.why: Find disease subtypes - useful for disease classification and phenotype mapping

tests.name: EFO Disease to Parent Terms
tests.why: Navigate to broader disease categories for meta-analysis grouping

tests.name: GO Term to Genes (via Ensembl)
tests.why: Find all human genes with specific GO annotation - reverse lookup for function

tests.name: Protein to All Pathways
tests.why: Get complete pathway context for protein - systems biology analysis

tests.name: Protein to Activation Pathways
tests.why: Focus on activation mechanisms - key for understanding signal transduction

tests.name: Protein to Regulation Pathways
tests.why: Find regulatory pathways - important for feedback loops and control mechanisms

tests.name: Gene to STRING Interactions
tests.why: Build protein interaction networks - essential for network biology and target discovery

tests.name: Molecule Search by SMILES
tests.why: Convert chemical structure to database ID - critical for cheminformatics

tests.name: Molecule Search by InChI Key
tests.why: Standard chemical identifier lookup - for database integration

tests.name: ChEMBL Molecule to PubChem (UniChem)
tests.why: ChEMBL molecule to PubChem compound via UniChem mappings - enables disease→compound queries

tests.name: EFO Disease to ChEMBL to PubChem
tests.why: Disease → ChEMBL molecule → PubChem compound path for drug discovery

tests.name: ZINC ID Search (Virtual Screening)
tests.why: Search by ZINC database ID for virtual screening compound lookup

tests.name: Gene to ENA mRNA Sequences
tests.why: Get mRNA sequences for cloning and expression studies

tests.name: Gene to RefSeq IDs
tests.why: Map to NCBI RefSeq - standard reference sequences for genomics

tests.name: Entrez Gene to Ensembl
tests.why: Convert NCBI Gene IDs to Ensembl - cross-database integration

tests.name: Entrez Gene to GO Terms
tests.why: Functional annotation from Entrez Gene ID - legacy data integration

tests.name: Protein to IntAct Interactions
tests.why: Find experimentally validated protein-protein interactions for network analysis

tests.name: UniProt to IntAct Interactions
tests.why: Direct UniProt to IntAct mapping - used in IntAct path for protein interaction queries

tests.name: Gene to Protein Interactions via UniProt
tests.why: Map genes to interaction partners - essential for pathway and network biology

tests.name: Protein to Interaction Partners Details
tests.why: Get detailed protein information for interaction partners

tests.name: IntAct to GO Biological Process
tests.why: Functional annotation of interaction partners - pathway context for networks

tests.name: ChEBI to IntAct Interactions (Protein-Small Molecule)
tests.why: Find protein interactions with small molecules - essential for drug-target analysis

tests.name: ChEBI to Interacting Proteins via IntAct
tests.why: Map small molecules to their protein targets - drug mechanism of action

tests.name: RNAcentral to IntAct Interactions (Protein-RNA)
tests.why: Find protein-RNA interactions - critical for RNA biology and RBP analysis

tests.name: RNAcentral to Interacting Proteins via IntAct
tests.why: Map RNAs to their binding proteins - essential for RNA-protein complex analysis

tests.name: IntAct Interaction Lookup by ID
tests.why: Direct lookup of interaction details by IntAct ID

tests.name: Protein to Small Molecule Partners via IntAct
tests.why: Find small molecules that interact with a protein - ligand discovery

tests.name: Ensembl Gene to IntAct Interactions (Protein-Gene)
tests.why: Find protein-gene interactions where a gene product interacts with proteins - captures regulatory interactions

tests.name: Transcript to IntAct Interactions (mRNA-miRNA)
tests.why: Find mRNA-miRNA interactions where Ensembl transcripts interact with miRNAs (RNAcentral)

tests.name: Transcript to miRNA Partners via IntAct
tests.why: Map mRNA transcripts to their regulatory miRNAs via IntAct interactions

tests.name: Gene to Bgee Expression Data
tests.why: Get tissue-specific expression patterns across anatomical structures

tests.name: Tissue to Expressed Genes via Bgee
tests.why: Find genes expressed in specific tissue - tissue marker discovery

tests.name: Cell Type to Expressed Genes
tests.why: Identify genes expressed in specific cell types - single-cell markers

tests.name: Gene to Tissues Where Expressed
tests.why: Map gene to anatomical locations - expression atlas

tests.name: Gene to Cell Types Where Expressed
tests.why: Identify cell-type specific expression - scRNA-seq validation

tests.name: UBERON Parent Tissues
tests.why: Navigate anatomy hierarchy upward - from specific to general structures

tests.name: UBERON Child Structures
tests.why: Navigate anatomy hierarchy downward - find sub-structures

tests.name: Cell Ontology Parents
tests.why: Navigate cell type hierarchy - from specific to general cell types

tests.name: Cell Ontology Children
tests.why: Find cell subtypes - detailed cell type classification

tests.name: Reactome Pathway Lookup
tests.why: Get curated pathway information with participants and hierarchy

tests.name: Protein to Reactome Pathways
tests.why: Find all pathways involving protein - systems biology analysis

tests.name: Reactome Pathway Parents
tests.why: Navigate pathway hierarchy upward - find broader pathway categories

tests.name: Reactome Pathway Children
tests.why: Navigate pathway hierarchy downward - find sub-pathways

tests.name: Reactome to Proteins
tests.why: Get all proteins involved in pathway - network reconstruction

tests.name: Rhea Reaction Lookup
tests.why: Get biochemical reaction details with substrates and products

tests.name: ChEBI Compound to Rhea Reactions
tests.why: Find reactions involving specific chemical compound

tests.name: Disease Trait to GWAS SNPs via EFO
tests.why: Find genetic variants associated with disease - reverse lookup from phenotype

tests.name: Study to Associated SNPs
tests.why: Get all SNP associations from GWAS study publication

tests.name: SwissLipids Lipid Lookup
tests.why: Get lipid structure and biological context

tests.name: AlphaFold Structure Lookup
tests.why: Get predicted 3D structure for protein

tests.name: Gene to AlphaFold via UniProt
tests.why: Map genes to AlphaFold predicted structures for drug discovery - structure-based drug design workflow

tests.name: UniProt to AlphaFold Structure
tests.why: Direct UniProt to AlphaFold mapping for structural analysis

tests.name: AlphaFold Attributes Validation
tests.why: TP53 AlphaFold structure should have pLDDT confidence metrics

tests.name: Complete Network: Gene to Interactions to Pathways
tests.why: Build complete functional network - interaction partners and their pathways

tests.name: Gene to BioGRID Interactions via Entrez
tests.why: Map genes to BioGRID protein-protein interactions - critical for network analysis and drug discovery

tests.name: Entrez Gene to BioGRID Interactions
tests.why: Direct Entrez Gene ID to BioGRID mapping for interaction network analysis

tests.name: BioGRID Interaction Lookup by ID
tests.why: Direct lookup of BioGRID interaction entry by BioGRID ID

tests.name: BioGRID to UniProt Interactors
tests.why: Get UniProt IDs for BioGRID interaction partners - enables protein feature analysis of interactors

tests.name: UniProt to BioGRID Interactions
tests.why: Direct UniProt to BioGRID mapping for drug target interaction enrichment

tests.name: Multi-hop: Tissue to Genes to Functions
tests.why: Tissue-specific functional enrichment - find functions of genes in tissues

tests.name: Gene to ClinVar Variants via Ensembl
tests.why: Map genes to clinical variants - critical for clinical genetics and variant interpretation. NOTE: HGNC is embedded in Ensembl, so use ensembl path not hgnc.

tests.name: Ensembl Gene ID to ClinVar Variants
tests.why: Direct Ensembl gene ID to ClinVar mapping for clinical research

tests.name: Cancer Gene Panel to ClinVar
tests.why: Batch query cancer genes to ClinVar for clinical panel analysis (Lynch syndrome genes)

tests.name: Gene to ClinVar to UniProt Features
tests.why: Multi-hop: gene to clinical variants with protein feature context

tests.name: ClinVar Variant to MONDO Disease
tests.why: Map ClinVar variants to MONDO disease ontology for disease classification

tests.name: MONDO Disease to ClinVar Variants
tests.why: Find all ClinVar variants associated with a specific MONDO disease term

tests.name: ClinVar Variant to MeSH Terms
tests.why: Map ClinVar variants to MeSH disease descriptors for medical literature search

tests.name: ClinVar Variant to HPO Phenotypes
tests.why: Map ClinVar variants to Human Phenotype Ontology terms for phenotype analysis

tests.name: ClinVar Variant to OMIM
tests.why: Map ClinVar variants to OMIM/MIM entries for Mendelian disease research

tests.name: GO Kinase Activity to Proteins
tests.why: Find proteins with kinase activity - prime drug targets for cancer therapy

tests.name: GO DNA Repair to Proteins
tests.why: DNA repair pathway proteins - targets for PARP inhibitors and cancer therapy

tests.name: Reactome Metabolism to Proteins
tests.why: Metabolism pathway proteins for metabolic disease drug targets

tests.name: Reactome Signal Transduction to Drug Targets
tests.why: Signal transduction pathway to druggable protein components

tests.name: Brain-Specific Expression to Proteins
tests.why: Brain-expressed genes to proteins for neurological drug discovery

tests.name: GO Apoptosis to Protein Domains
tests.why: Apoptosis pathway genes to domain annotations for structural analysis

tests.name: Reactome Immune System to Interactions
tests.why: Immune pathway proteins to interaction networks for immunotherapy targets

tests.name: EFO Heart Disease to GWAS
tests.why: Heart disease trait to GWAS SNP associations

tests.name: EFO Diabetes to GWAS
tests.why: Diabetes trait to GWAS SNP associations for genetic risk analysis

tests.name: Cancer Genes to Orthologs Batch
tests.why: Batch query cancer genes to orthologs for model organism studies

tests.name: Gene to dbSNP Variants
tests.why: Map genes to dbSNP variants for genetic variation analysis - core SNP discovery workflow

tests.name: Ensembl Gene ID to dbSNP
tests.why: Direct Ensembl gene ID to dbSNP variant mapping

tests.name: dbSNP Variant Lookup by rsID
tests.why: Direct lookup of dbSNP variants by rs ID - essential for SNP annotation

tests.name: dbSNP to ClinVar via Gene
tests.why: Find clinical significance of dbSNP variants via gene to ClinVar mapping

tests.name: Gene Symbol to Entrez to UniProt
tests.why: Map gene symbols via NCBI Entrez Gene to UniProt proteins - alternative to Ensembl route

tests.name: Gene Symbol to Entrez to GO
tests.why: Gene function via NCBI gene2go annotations - comprehensive GO mapping

tests.name: Gene Symbol to Entrez to RefSeq
tests.why: Map to NCBI RefSeq reference sequences for gene and transcript data

tests.name: Gene Symbol to Entrez Orthologs
tests.why: Find orthologous genes across species via NCBI gene orthologs

tests.name: Gene Symbol to Entrez Related Genes
tests.why: Find functionally related genes via NCBI gene groups

tests.name: Gene Symbol to Entrez Neighboring Genes
tests.why: Find genomically neighboring genes via NCBI gene neighbors

tests.name: PubChem Compound Lookup
tests.why: Search PubChem compounds by CID - rich chemical attributes (SMILES, InChI, FDA status)

tests.name: RefSeq mRNA Lookup (versioned)
tests.why: Search RefSeq mRNA with version - full attributes (type, status, symbol, description)

tests.name: RefSeq mRNA Lookup (unversioned)
tests.why: Search RefSeq mRNA without version - works but no attributes

tests.name: RefSeq Protein Lookup
tests.why: Search RefSeq protein accessions directly

tests.name: Entrez Gene to Ensembl to ClinVar
tests.why: Multi-hop from Entrez gene to clinical variants via Ensembl

tests.name: RefSeq to Entrez
tests.why: RefSeq accessions should map back to Entrez Gene IDs - needs outgoing links from RefSeq

tests.name: RefSeq to UniProt
tests.why: RefSeq to UniProt mapping - needs gene_refseq_uniprotkb_collab.gz integration

tests.name: PubChem to BioActivity
tests.why: PubChem compound to bioactivity data (assay results)

tests.name: PubChem to Gene Target via Activity
tests.why: PubChem compound to gene targets via bioactivity data

tests.name: PubChem to Protein Target via Activity
tests.why: PubChem compound to protein targets via bioactivity data. NOTE: Can be slow for compounds with many activities (e.g., aspirin has 3689 cross-refs)

tests.name: PubChem to HMDB
tests.why: PubChem compound to HMDB metabolite cross-reference

tests.name: PubChem to Patent
tests.why: PubChem compound to patent cross-reference

tests.name: PubChem to ChEMBL Molecule
tests.why: PubChem compound to ChEMBL molecule cross-reference (extracted from synonyms like CHEMBL25)

tests.name: PubChem to BindingDB
tests.why: PubChem compound to BindingDB cross-reference (extracted from BDBM synonyms)

tests.name: PubChem to Patent Compound (SureChEMBL)
tests.why: PubChem compound to SureChEMBL patent compound cross-reference (extracted from SCHEMBL synonyms)

tests.name: PubChem to CAS
tests.why: PubChem compound to CAS Registry Number cross-reference

tests.name: Entrez Gene ID Direct to Ensembl
tests.why: Map numeric Entrez Gene IDs directly to Ensembl - WORKS! 672=BRCA1, 7157=TP53

tests.name: Aspirin Attributes
tests.why: Validate PubChem compound attributes for aspirin (CID 2244)

tests.name: Aspirin External Database Identifiers
tests.why: Validate PubChem external database identifiers extracted from synonyms for aspirin (CID 2244). NOTE: These fields may be empty if synonyms don't contain these identifiers.

tests.name: Entrez Human BRCA1 Attributes
tests.why: Validate Entrez gene attributes for human BRCA1 (Gene ID 672)

tests.name: RefSeq BRCA1 Attributes
tests.why: Validate RefSeq attributes for BRCA1 mRNA

tests.name: BRCA1 ClinVar Count
tests.why: BRCA1 should have many ClinVar variants (>100)

tests.name: TP53 GO Count
tests.why: TP53 should have many GO annotations (>50)

tests.name: Aspirin Cross-ref Count
tests.why: Aspirin should have many cross-references (>1000) including bioactivities

tests.name: TP53 to P04637 Known Fact
tests.why: TP53 gene should encode UniProt protein P04637

tests.name: EGFR to P00533 Known Fact
tests.why: EGFR gene should encode UniProt protein P00533

tests.name: BRCA1 to P38398 Known Fact
tests.why: BRCA1 gene should encode UniProt protein P38398

tests.name: Aspirin to HMDB Known Fact
tests.why: Aspirin should link to HMDB metabolite database

tests.name: Type 2 Diabetes MONDO to GWAS Genes
tests.why: Find genes associated with Type 2 Diabetes via GWAS studies - disease genetics workflow

tests.name: Disease to GWAS Genes via EFO (Human)
tests.why: Full disease to human genes path via GWAS studies. MONDO→EFO direct mapping is sparse; go via gwas_study instead.

tests.name: Glioblastoma Text to GWAS Genes
tests.why: Drug discovery workflow: disease text to genes via GWAS studies - tests text-based disease lookup. MONDO:0005044 is main glioblastoma (not subtypes like MONDO:0000457)

tests.name: Disease to ClinVar Genes via MONDO (Human)
tests.why: Full disease to human genes path via ClinVar - used in dbSNP path for gene retrieval

tests.name: Glioblastoma Text to ClinVar Genes
tests.why: Drug discovery workflow: disease text to genes via ClinVar - tests text-based disease lookup. MONDO:0005044 is main glioblastoma (not subtypes like MONDO:0000457)

tests.name: Protein to Pathogenic ClinVar Variants
tests.why: Filter ClinVar variants to only pathogenic classifications - critical for clinical variant interpretation and identifying disease-causing mutations

tests.name: Gene to Pathogenic ClinVar Variants
tests.why: Find pathogenic variants for cancer genes - essential for clinical genetics panels and hereditary cancer testing

tests.name: Gene to Likely Pathogenic ClinVar Variants
tests.why: Find likely pathogenic variants - important for variant classification in clinical reports

tests.name: Gene to SNV ClinVar Variants
tests.why: Filter ClinVar to only single nucleotide variants - useful for SNP-focused analysis

tests.name: Gene to Deletion ClinVar Variants
tests.why: Filter ClinVar to deletion variants - important for detecting loss-of-function mutations

tests.name: ClinVar Pathogenic Variant Attributes Validation
tests.why: RB1 pathogenic variants should have correct classification attribute - filter ensures only pathogenic variants returned

tests.name: JAK2 Advanced Clinical Trial Drugs
tests.why: Find JAK2 drugs in advanced clinical trials (Phase 3/4) - full drug discovery chain. NOTE: This is a long chain query and may be slow (~60s)

tests.name: Disease Text to AlphaFold Structures
tests.why: Drug discovery workflow: disease text to AlphaFold structures for structure-based drug design

tests.name: Disease Text to BioGRID Interactions
tests.why: Drug discovery workflow: disease text to protein interactions for network-based target discovery

tests.name: Disease Text to dbSNP Variants
tests.why: Drug discovery workflow: disease text to genetic variants for pharmacogenomics

tests.name: Disease Text to IntAct Interactions
tests.why: Drug discovery workflow: disease text to experimentally validated protein interactions

tests.name: Therapeutic Antibody Lookup
tests.why: Therapeutic antibodies (TheraSAbDab) should be directly queryable by INN name

tests.name: Therapeutic Antibody to Target Genes
tests.why: Therapeutic antibodies should link to their target genes via composite target notation splitting (e.g., ITGA2B/CD41 -> ITGA2B)

tests.name: Structural Antibody via PDB
tests.why: PDB structures should link to SAbDab antibody entries for structural antibody data

tests.name: Gene Target to Therapeutic Antibodies
tests.why: Find all therapeutic antibodies targeting a specific gene - critical for competitive landscape analysis and drug discovery

tests.name: Antibody Attributes Validation
tests.why: Bevacizumab should have correct attributes including target VEGFA and therapeutic source

tests.name: Antibody to EFO Disease Ontology
tests.why: Therapeutic antibodies with indications should map to EFO disease ontology. TRASTUZUMAB has no EFO xrefs (only ensembl, mondo).

tests.name: Antibody to MONDO Disease Ontology
tests.why: Therapeutic antibodies with indications should map to MONDO disease ontology via text lookup

tests.name: EFO Disease to Therapeutic Antibodies
tests.why: Find therapeutic antibodies approved for specific diseases - drug repurposing and competitive analysis

tests.name: MONDO Disease to Therapeutic Antibodies
tests.why: Find therapeutic antibodies for MONDO diseases - enables disease-centric drug discovery

tests.name: Clinical Trial Lookup
tests.why: Clinical trials should be directly queryable by NCT ID

tests.name: Clinical Trial to MONDO Disease
tests.why: Clinical trials with disease conditions should map to MONDO ontology via text lookup

tests.name: MONDO Disease to Clinical Trials
tests.why: MONDO disease terms should link to clinical trials studying that disease - enables disease-centric trial discovery

tests.name: Disease Text Search to Clinical Trials
tests.why: Disease names should find clinical trials via text search - primary discovery workflow

tests.name: Disease to Clinical Trials via MONDO
tests.why: Find clinical trials for disease terms via MONDO ontology mapping - handles partial names

tests.name: Clinical Trial to Gene Targets via MONDO
tests.why: Multi-hop: find gene targets for diseases studied in clinical trials

tests.name: Clinical Trial Attributes Validation
tests.why: Clinical trial should have correct attributes including phase and study type

tests.name: Clinical Trial MONDO Count Validation
tests.why: NCT06777108 (Hypertension trial) should have at least 1 MONDO mapping

tests.name: Gene to PubChem Compounds via Bioactivity
tests.why: Find PubChem compounds with bioactivity against gene targets - complementary to ChEMBL

tests.name: Gene to FDA-Approved PubChem Compounds
tests.why: Find FDA-approved drugs from PubChem with bioactivity against gene targets

tests.name: PubChem Compound to Gene Targets
tests.why: Find gene targets for a PubChem compound

tests.name: PubChem Compound to Protein Targets
tests.why: Find protein targets for a PubChem compound

tests.name: PubChem to HMDB Metabolites
tests.why: Cross-reference PubChem compounds to HMDB metabolite database

tests.name: PubChem to ChEMBL Cross-Reference
tests.why: Cross-reference PubChem to ChEMBL for combined drug data

tests.name: PubChem to Literature References
tests.why: Find PubMed literature references for PubChem compounds (63k+ for aspirin)

tests.name: PubChem to Patents
tests.why: Find patent information for PubChem compounds

tests.name: PubChem to BindingDB
tests.why: Cross-reference to BindingDB for binding affinity data (Ki, IC50)

tests.name: PubChem to CTD (Toxicogenomics)
tests.why: Map compounds to CTD for toxicogenomics data (gene-chemical-disease relationships)

tests.name: PubChem to PharmGKB
tests.why: Cross-reference to PharmGKB for pharmacogenomics annotations

tests.name: Protein to PubChem Activities
tests.why: Find all PubChem bioactivity data for a protein target

tests.name: PubChem Activity to Gene Target
tests.why: Map PubChem bioactivity to gene targets

tests.name: MONDO Disease to CTD via MeSH Bridge
tests.why: Disease to compounds via MeSH bridge - reliable workaround for MONDO->CTD (BUG-010)

tests.name: MeSH Disease to CTD Compounds
tests.why: Direct MeSH to CTD to PubChem - high-volume toxicogenomics (5000+ for D001943)

tests.name: CTD to PubChem Compound
tests.why: CTD chemical entries to PubChem compound IDs

tests.name: CTD to EFO Disease Ontology
tests.why: CTD chemicals map to EFO disease ontology via disease associations. D001241=Aspirin, D000082=Acetaminophen. D003924 was wrong (disease MeSH, not CTD chemical).

tests.name: dbSNP to GWAS Association
tests.why: SNP to GWAS association is a core variant analysis use case

tests.name: Gene to PharmGKB Clinical Annotations
tests.why: Gene to clinical annotations for pharmacogenomic dosing guidelines

tests.name: Drug to CPIC Guidelines
tests.why: CPIC dosing guideline retrieval for pharmacogenomics

tests.name: Protein to AlphaMissense Predictions
tests.why: Get all AI-predicted pathogenic missense variants for a protein

tests.name: GWAS Study to SNPs
tests.why: Get all SNPs from a GWAS study

tests.name: ClinVar to Disease (MONDO)
tests.why: Variant to disease association lookup

tests.name: Disease to Therapeutic Antibodies
tests.why: Find approved or in-development therapeutic antibodies for diseases

tests.name: DrugCentral via UniProt
tests.why: Find all approved drugs targeting a specific protein

tests.name: ChEMBL to DrugCentral
tests.why: Map ChEMBL compounds to DrugCentral approved drugs via InChIKey lookup. CHEMBL1201607 (Natalizumab) is a biotherapeutic with no DrugCentral mapping.

tests.name: PubChem to DrugCentral
tests.why: Map PubChem compounds to DrugCentral approved drugs via InChIKey lookup

tests.name: Huntington Disease Gene Associations
tests.why: Huntington disease should have strong GenCC gene associations

tests.name: Full ChEMBL Chain - BRAF
tests.why: BRAF is a validated oncology target with many drugs - test full 7-step chain

tests.name: Gene to Tissues via Bgee
tests.why: Map gene to all tissues where it is expressed

tests.name: Gene to Cell Types via Bgee
tests.why: Map gene to cell types with expression

tests.name: Cell Type to CellXGene Datasets
tests.why: Find single-cell datasets containing specific cell types

tests.name: Tissue to CellXGene Datasets
tests.why: Find single-cell datasets for specific tissues

tests.name: Disease to CellXGene Datasets
tests.why: Find disease-specific scRNA-seq datasets

tests.name: Gene to SCXA Experiments
tests.why: Map gene to Single Cell Expression Atlas experiments

tests.name: Cell Type to SCXA Experiments
tests.why: Find SCXA experiments with specific cell types

tests.name: Gene to SCXA Direct
tests.why: Simpler path: gene to Single Cell Expression Atlas datasets

tests.name: CellXGene to Disease (Reverse)
tests.why: Reverse mapping from CellXGene dataset to associated diseases

tests.name: CellXGene to Cell Types (Reverse)
tests.why: Reverse mapping from CellXGene dataset to cell types present

tests.name: CellXGene to Tissues (Reverse)
tests.why: Reverse mapping from CellXGene dataset to tissues sampled

tests.name: Tissue to CellXGene (Pancreas)
tests.why: Find scRNA-seq datasets for pancreas tissue (13 datasets)

tests.name: Pathway Hierarchy Child
tests.why: Test pathway hierarchy navigation downward

tests.name: Pathway Hierarchy Parent
tests.why: Test pathway hierarchy navigation upward

tests.name: Reactome to UniProt Direct
tests.why: Map Reactome pathways directly to participating proteins

tests.name: Reactome Pathway to Genes
tests.why: Map Reactome pathways directly to participating genes (41 genes for HDR pathway)

tests.name: GO to Proteins (Reverse Lookup)
tests.why: Find proteins with specific molecular function

tests.name: Protein to STRING Network
tests.why: Map UniProt to STRING for network analysis

tests.name: STRING to Protein Partners
tests.why: Map STRING ID back to UniProt interaction partners

tests.name: Protein Interaction Network via IntAct
tests.why: Find protein interaction partners via IntAct

tests.name: Protein Interaction Network via BioGRID
tests.why: Find BRCA1 interaction partners via BioGRID

tests.name: MONDO Parent Traversal
tests.why: Verify upward traversal in MONDO disease hierarchy

tests.name: MONDO Child Enumeration
tests.why: Verify downward traversal to find disease subtypes

tests.name: HPO Parent Traversal
tests.why: Verify upward traversal in HPO phenotype hierarchy

tests.name: HPO Child Enumeration
tests.why: Verify downward traversal to find phenotype subtypes

tests.name: HPO to OMIM Disease Mapping
tests.why: Map phenotypes to OMIM diseases - from phenotype.hpoa annotations (~160K links)

tests.name: HPO to Orphanet Disease Mapping
tests.why: Map phenotypes to Orphanet rare diseases - from phenotype.hpoa annotations (~116K links)

tests.name: OMIM to HPO Reverse Mapping
tests.why: Reverse mapping: find phenotypes associated with an OMIM disease

tests.name: Orphanet to HPO Reverse Mapping
tests.why: Reverse mapping: find phenotypes associated with an Orphanet disease. Orphanet 166 is a Category type with no HPO phenotypes.

tests.name: EFO to MONDO Mapping
tests.why: Verify cross-ontology mapping from EFO traits to MONDO diseases

tests.name: MONDO to GenCC Gene Curations
tests.why: Verify disease to gene-disease curation mappings

tests.name: MONDO to GWAS Associations
tests.why: Verify disease to GWAS catalog associations

tests.name: MeSH Parent Traversal
tests.why: Navigate MeSH tree hierarchy upward - find broader descriptor categories

tests.name: MeSH Child Enumeration
tests.why: Navigate MeSH tree hierarchy downward - find narrower descriptors

tests.name: MeSH Entry Lookup
tests.why: Basic MeSH descriptor lookup by ID

tests.name: MeSH Descriptor Attributes
tests.why: Verify MeSH descriptor has expected attributes (name, tree numbers, scope note)

tests.name: MeSH Parent Count Validation
tests.why: D000001 (Calcimycin) should have at least 2 parent descriptors from its tree numbers

tests.name: MeSH Breast Cancer Subtypes
tests.why: D001943 (Breast Neoplasms) should have 8 child descriptors including HER2+ and TNBC

tests.name: MeSH Breast Cancer Parents
tests.why: D001943 (Breast Neoplasms) should have 2 parent descriptors

tests.name: MeSH Breast Cancer Child Count
tests.why: D001943 (Breast Neoplasms) should have exactly 8 child subtypes

tests.name: Trial to Diseases (High Count)
tests.why: NCT00000466 has 106 associated MONDO disease terms - tests high-volume mappings

tests.name: Diabetes Clinical Trials
tests.why: MONDO:0005148 (Type 2 Diabetes) has 12,000+ clinical trials

tests.name: Rare Disease FDA-Approved Drugs
tests.why: Full rare disease to FDA-approved drugs pipeline (54 drugs for Huntington's target)

tests.name: ALS Gene Targets
tests.why: ALS (MONDO:0004976) has 33 GenCC-curated genes

tests.name: Therapeutic Antibodies for ALS
tests.why: Find therapeutic antibodies in development (4 for ALS including Tocilizumab)

tests.name: DNA Repair Pathway Genes
tests.why: R-HSA-73894 (DNA Repair) contains 138 genes

tests.name: Apoptosis Sub-Pathways
tests.why: R-HSA-109581 (Apoptosis) has 4 child pathways

tests.name: SwissLipids to Proteins
tests.why: Cholesterol (SLM:000000287) links to 60 proteins including CYP enzymes

tests.name: Rhea Enzymatic Reactions
tests.why: EGFR (P00533) links to RHEA:10596 tyrosine kinase reaction

tests.name: Cross-Species Orthologs
tests.why: TP53 has orthologs in mouse, rat, zebrafish, fly

tests.name: Pharmacogene Clinical Annotations (CYP2D6)
tests.why: CYP2D6 has 75+ clinical annotations with evidence levels

tests.name: Drug Metabolism Pathway
tests.why: Warfarin has 2 pathways (PK + PD) with CYP genes

tests.name: PubChem to PharmGKB Bidirectional
tests.why: Warfarin PubChem CID 54678486 links to PA451906

tests.name: Pharmacogenomic SNP GWAS
tests.why: rs1799853 (CYP2C9*2) has 19 GWAS associations for drug response

tests.name: COVID-19 Single-Cell Datasets
tests.why: COVID-19 (MONDO:0100096) has 63 scRNA-seq datasets

tests.name: Alzheimer's Single-Cell Datasets
tests.why: Alzheimer's (MONDO:0004975) has 25 scRNA-seq datasets

tests.name: Rare Cell Type Datasets (Purkinje)
tests.why: Even rare cell types like Purkinje cells have datasets (16)

tests.name: Bgee Expression Breadth
tests.why: TP53 has 223 present calls across 281 conditions

tests.name: Human-Specific Pathogenic Variants
tests.why: Filter for human-only pathogenic variants (avoids mouse/rat orthologs)

tests.name: HPO to ClinVar Variants
tests.why: Phenotype to variants works (150+ for seizures HP:0001250)

tests.name: PubChem FDA Approved Filter
tests.why: Filter for FDA-approved drugs only

tests.name: PubChem Compound Type Drug Filter
tests.why: Filter for drug compounds vs bioactive/literature

tests.name: PubChem Lipinski H-bond Donors
tests.why: Lipinski Rule of 5 - H-bond donors filter works

tests.name: PubChem Rotatable Bonds Filter
tests.why: Drug-likeness filter for molecular flexibility

tests.name: ChEMBL Phase 4 Approved Drugs
tests.why: Filter for approved drugs (phase 4) - 157 for EGFR

tests.name: ChEMBL Phase 3+ Drugs
tests.why: Filter for Phase 3 and above drugs

tests.name: ClinVar Pathogenic Filter
tests.why: Filter for pathogenic variants only - 150+ for BRCA1

tests.name: ClinVar Likely Pathogenic Filter
tests.why: Filter for likely pathogenic variants

tests.name: AlphaMissense Class Filter
tests.why: Filter for likely pathogenic missense variants - 150+ for P04637

tests.name: AlphaMissense Score Filter
tests.why: Filter by pathogenicity score > 0.8

tests.name: PharmGKB Level 1A Evidence
tests.why: Filter for highest evidence clinical annotations - 32 for CYP2D6

tests.name: PharmGKB CPIC Guidelines
tests.why: Filter for CPIC dosing guidelines

tests.name: GenCC Definitive Evidence
tests.why: Filter for definitive gene-disease evidence

tests.name: GenCC Strong Evidence
tests.why: Filter for strong gene-disease evidence - 4 for TP53

tests.name: GenCC Autosomal Dominant
tests.why: Filter by inheritance mode

tests.name: GWAS Genome-Wide Significant
tests.why: Filter for genome-wide significant associations (p<5e-8)

tests.name: GO Biological Process Filter
tests.why: Filter for biological process GO terms - 120 for TP53

tests.name: GO Molecular Function Filter
tests.why: Filter for molecular function GO terms

tests.name: GO Cellular Component Filter
tests.why: Filter for cellular component GO terms - 18 for TP53

tests.name: UniProt Reviewed Swiss-Prot
tests.why: Filter for curated Swiss-Prot entries only

tests.name: InterPro Domain Filter
tests.why: Filter for protein domains - 20 for EGFR

tests.name: InterPro Family Filter
tests.why: Filter for protein families

tests.name: Antibody Therapeutic Type
tests.why: Filter for therapeutic antibodies - 130 for EGFR

tests.name: Ensembl Human Genome Filter
tests.why: Filter for human genes only

tests.name: UniProt Feature Modified Residue
tests.why: Filter for phosphorylation sites - 74 for EGFR

tests.name: UniProt Feature Disulfide Bond
tests.why: Filter for disulfide bonds - 100 for EGFR

tests._comment: === BROKEN FILTER TESTS (2026-02-01) - Document for fixing ===

tests.name: Clinical Trials Phase Filter (FIXED)
tests.why: FIXED: Added CEL registration for clinical_trials

tests.name: AlphaFold Global Metric Filter (FIXED - use .0)
tests.why: FIXED: Use .0 suffix for float comparisons

tests.name: AlphaFold Mean PAE Filter
tests.why: Filter for low prediction error structures

tests.name: Bgee Max Expression Score Filter (FIXED)
tests.why: FIXED: Use top-level max_expression_score instead of nested field

tests.name: Bgee Gold Quality Count Filter
tests.why: Filter for genes with many high-quality expression calls

tests.name: Bgee Average Expression Score Filter
tests.why: Filter for genes with high average expression (TP53 has 77.6)

tests.name: Bgee Expression Breadth Filter
tests.why: Filter for ubiquitously expressed genes (TP53 is ubiquitous)

tests.name: PubChem Molecular Weight Filter (FIXED - use .0)
tests.why: FIXED: Use .0 suffix for float comparisons (Lipinski rule)

tests.name: PubChem LogP Filter
tests.why: Lipinski Rule of 5 - lipophilicity filter

tests.name: PubChem TPSA Filter
tests.why: Lipinski extension - polar surface area filter

tests.name: GWAS pvalue_mlog Filter (FIXED - use .0)
tests.why: FIXED: Use .0 suffix for float comparisons

tests.name: Clinical Trials Phase 3 Filter (FIXED)
tests.why: FIXED: clinical_trials CEL now registered. Phase values are UPPERCASE

tests.name: Clinical Trials Recruiting Filter
tests.why: Filter for actively recruiting trials

tests.name: HPO to MONDO Reverse (KNOWN BUG)
tests.why: Document known reverse mapping failure (BUG-005)

tests.name: MONDO to CTD Direct (BUG-010 - Use MeSH Bridge)
tests.why: Direct MONDO->CTD fails. WORKAROUND: Use >>mondo>>mesh>>ctd>>pubchem instead

tests.name: Reactome to UniProt Direct (BUG-003 - Needs Reprocess)
tests.why: Direct pathway->protein fails. WORKAROUND: Use >>reactome>>ensembl>>uniprot

tests._comment: New use cases discovered by parallel agents exploring ncRNA, toxicogenomics, MSigDB, AlphaMissense, rare diseases, and complex pipelines

tests.name: miRNA Drug Target Discovery (ChEMBL)
tests.why: Find compounds tested against oncogenic miRNAs - 150+ for miR-21, 36 for miR-122 (HCV target). MIR155 has no ChEMBL target component mapping.

tests.name: miRNA Tissue Expression via Bgee
tests.why: Find tissues where miRNAs are expressed - 12 tissues for miR-21

tests.name: lncRNA Pharmacogenomic Status
tests.why: Check if lncRNAs are VIP pharmacogenes - MALAT1 is recognized

tests.name: miRNA Cross-Species Conservation
tests.why: Find miRNA orthologs across species - 78 for 3 miRNAs

tests.name: lncRNA ClinVar Variants
tests.why: Find clinical variants in lncRNA genes - 38 for HOTAIR. MALAT1 resolves to zebrafish first and has no ClinVar entries.

tests.name: CTD Chemical to Gene Targets
tests.why: Find genes affected by chemical exposure - 24 for Acetaminophen

tests.name: CTD Chemical to Pathways (4 hops)
tests.why: Map toxic chemicals to affected pathways - 124 pathways for Acetaminophen

tests.name: Disease to Toxic Chemicals via MeSH
tests.why: Find chemicals associated with diseases - 150+ for breast neoplasms

tests.name: BindingDB to PubChem Cross-Reference
tests.why: Map BindingDB entries to PubChem compounds

tests.name: Gene to All MSigDB Gene Sets
tests.why: Find all gene sets containing a gene - 150+ for TP53

tests.name: Gene to Hallmark Gene Sets
tests.why: Filter to Hallmark collection only - 4 for TP53, 5 for CASP3

tests.name: Gene to Curated Gene Sets (C2)
tests.why: Filter to curated pathways (KEGG, Reactome, BioCarta) - 98 for BRCA1

tests.name: Gene Set to Member Genes
tests.why: Extract genes from a gene set - 161 genes from HALLMARK_APOPTOSIS

tests.name: HGNC ID to MSigDB Gene Sets
tests.why: Proper HGNC IDs (HGNC:####) should resolve to gene sets, not just gene symbols. Requires lookup database.

tests.name: MSigDB C5:GO Gene Set to GO Term
tests.why: C5:GO gene sets should map to their source GO term (BUG-014 fixed)

tests.name: MSigDB C5:HPO Gene Set to HPO Term
tests.why: C5:HPO gene sets should map to their source HPO term (BUG-015 fixed)

tests.name: GO Term to MSigDB Gene Sets
tests.why: Reverse mapping: find MSigDB gene sets derived from a GO term (BUG-014 fixed)

tests.name: HPO Term to MSigDB Gene Sets
tests.why: Reverse mapping: find MSigDB gene sets derived from an HPO term (BUG-015 fixed)

tests.name: MSigDB to Ensembl via HGNC (3-hop)
tests.why: Gene set to Ensembl genes via HGNC - multi-hop chain working

tests.name: Tissue to Drug Targets (6 hops)
tests.why: Discover drug targets from tissue-specific expression - 316 targets for brain

tests.name: Pathway to Drugs (8 hops)
tests.why: Find drugs targeting pathway components - 150+ for hemostasis

tests.name: Protein Interactome to AlphaFold (5 hops)
tests.why: Get AlphaFold structures for interaction partners - 171 for BRCA1

tests.name: GO Process to Approved Drugs (8 hops)
tests.why: Find approved drugs targeting proteins in biological process

tests.name: Phenotype to Genes via ClinVar
tests.why: Map clinical phenotypes to genes with pathogenic variants - 150+ for seizures

tests.name: Disease Genes to Model Organisms (4 hops)
tests.why: Find orthologs for disease genes - 49 for Huntington's

tests.name: RNACentral to IntAct Mapping (BUG-011 CLARIFIED)
tests.why: CLARIFIED: Works for IDs that exist in IntAct. URS000075D2D5 has no IntAct data (not a bug). URS000000B1C9 returns 150+ interactions.

tests.name: RNACentral to Ensembl Mapping (BUG-012 FIXED)
tests.why: FIXED: RNACentral now maps to Ensembl via id_mapping.tsv. Returns gene and transcript IDs.

tests.name: UniProt to BindingDB (BUG-013 FIXED)
tests.why: Protein to binding compounds - find compounds with measured binding affinity to a protein target. Fixed 2026-02-03: parser now extracts UniProt IDs from numbered chain columns.

tests.name: BindingDB to UniProt
tests.why: BindingDB entry to protein target - identify the protein target for a binding measurement

tests.name: GO to MSigDB Direct (BUG-014) - FIXED
tests.why: FIXED: GO term to MSigDB C5 gene sets now works. Moved working tests to gene_sets category.

tests.name: HPO to MSigDB Direct (BUG-015) - FIXED
tests.why: FIXED: HPO term to MSigDB C5 gene sets now works. HP:0001250 has no MSigDB gene set (expected).

tests.name: GenCC to HGNC Direct (BUG-016 FIXED)
tests.why: FIXED: GenCC now maps directly to HGNC via gene_curie field. BRCA1 returns 9 GenCC entries → HGNC:1100.

tests.name: MSigDB 4-Hop Chain (BUG-017) - VERIFIED WORKING
tests.why: Now works in full production build with all datasets indexed. M5902 (HALLMARK_APOPTOSIS) successfully maps through all 4 hops.
tests.note: Verified working 2026-02-04 after full production build

tests._comment: BUG-018: PharmGKB variant↔clinical, BUG-020: ClinVar→dbSNP, BUG-021: RNACentral text search
Plus new datasets: Orphanet rare diseases, MIM/OMIM, HMDB metabolites

tests.name: PharmGKB Variant to Clinical Annotation (BUG-018 FIXED)
tests.why: FIXED: Bidirectional xrefs between variant and clinical annotations via shared rsIDs. Critical for pharmacogenomic dosing decisions.

tests.name: PharmGKB Clinical to Variant Reverse (BUG-018 FIXED)
tests.why: FIXED: Reverse direction - from clinical annotation back to variant. Enables clinical-first pharmacogenomics queries.

tests.name: ClinVar to dbSNP Edge (BUG-020 FIXED)
tests.why: FIXED: ClinVar parser now creates dbSNP cross-references (was using wrong dataset key 'snp' instead of 'dbsnp'). Enables disease→variant→rsID pipelines.

tests.name: Disease to Pathogenic Variants with rsIDs (BUG-020 FIXED)
tests.why: FIXED: Full disease→ClinVar→dbSNP pipeline. Get variant rsIDs for a disease.

tests.name: RNACentral Search by lncRNA Gene Name
tests.why: FIXED BUG-021: Search RNACentral by gene name. HOTAIR returns URS000011D1F0 (lncRNA). Gene names indexed from id_mapping.tsv column 6.

tests.name: RNACentral Search by miRNA Name
tests.why: FIXED BUG-021: Search RNACentral by miRBase name. hsa-mir-21 returns URS00000AF93C (pre_miRNA, 32 species).

tests.name: RNACentral Search by RNA Type
tests.why: FIXED BUG-021: Search RNACentral by RNA type. RNA types indexed from FASTA descriptions.

tests.name: RNACentral to RefSeq Cross-Reference
tests.why: RNACentral to RefSeq mapping via id_mapping.tsv. Essential for NCBI-based workflows.

tests.name: RNACentral to HGNC Cross-Reference
tests.why: RNACentral to HGNC gene symbol mapping. Enables ncRNA to gene-centric queries.

tests.name: RNACentral to ENA Cross-Reference
tests.why: RNACentral to ENA accession mapping. Largest cross-reference set (~157M mappings).

tests.name: RNACentral to PDB Structure
tests.why: RNACentral RNA to 3D structure mapping. Find RNA structures in PDB.

tests.name: Orphanet Text Search
tests.why: Search Orphanet rare disease database by disease name. Marfan syndrome returns Orphanet ID 558.

tests.name: Orphanet to Disease Genes (Ensembl)
tests.why: Map Orphanet rare disease to associated genes via product6.xml gene associations. Only works for specific disease subtypes that have gene data (not parent/group disorders like 558).

tests.name: Orphanet to HPO Phenotypes
tests.why: Find phenotypes for a rare disease. Orphanet embeds HPO associations with frequency evidence (Obligate, Very frequent, Occasional).

tests.name: HPO Phenotype to Orphanet Rare Diseases
tests.why: Find rare diseases matching a phenotype. HP:0001250 (Seizure) returns 150+ rare diseases with frequency data.

tests.name: HPO Phenotype to MIM/OMIM Diseases
tests.why: Find Mendelian diseases for a phenotype via HPO-OMIM links. Returns entries with evidence codes.

tests.name: Orphanet to MONDO Disease Mapping
tests.why: Map Orphanet rare disease to MONDO ontology terms.

tests.name: Orphanet to MeSH Disease Mapping
tests.why: Map Orphanet rare disease to MeSH terms for literature search.

tests.name: Orphanet to MIM/OMIM Cross-Reference
tests.why: Map Orphanet rare disease to OMIM entries for Mendelian disease details.

tests.name: MIM to ClinVar Variants
tests.why: Find ClinVar variants associated with an OMIM Mendelian disease.

tests.name: MIM to HPO Phenotypes
tests.why: Find phenotypes associated with an OMIM disease entry.

tests.name: MONDO to Orphanet Rare Disease
tests.why: Map MONDO disease to Orphanet entry for rare disease classification and gene associations.

tests.name: Rare Disease Gene Discovery Pipeline
tests.why: Full pipeline: disease → rare disease classification → associated genes. MONDO:0007947 (Marfan) maps to orphanet subtypes 284963/558, subtype 284963 has ensembl gene FBN1.
tests.pending_rebuild: BUG-023: Fixed Orphanet prefix stripping in mondo.go - needs rebuild with --d mondo

tests.name: Phenotype to Rare Disease Genes Pipeline
tests.why: Phenotype-driven gene discovery for rare diseases via Orphanet. HP:0001250 (Seizure) maps through orphanet disorders to associated genes (~75 ensembl targets).

tests.name: Phenotype to Mendelian Disease Genes Pipeline
tests.why: Phenotype to OMIM to ClinVar to genes - Mendelian disease gene discovery pipeline.

tests.name: HMDB Text Search
tests.why: Search HMDB metabolite database by compound name.

tests.name: HMDB to PubChem Cross-Reference
tests.why: Map HMDB metabolite to PubChem compound for chemistry data.

tests.name: HMDB to ChEBI Cross-Reference
tests.why: Map HMDB metabolite to ChEBI ontology for chemical classification.

tests.name: HMDB to UniProt Enzyme
tests.why: Find enzymes/proteins associated with a metabolite.

tests.name: HPO Phenotype to HMDB Metabolites
tests.why: Find metabolites associated with a clinical phenotype. HP:0001250 (Seizure) returns 31 metabolites.

tests.name: HPO to MONDO via ClinVar Bridge
tests.why: Alternative to direct HPO→MONDO (limited to 579 terms). Via ClinVar returns 150+ MONDO diseases for HP:0001250.

tests.name: Gene to Patent Landscape via PubChem Bioactivity
tests.why: Map gene through protein and PubChem bioactivity to patent filings (5 hops)

tests.name: Disease to Patent via Clinical Trials
tests.why: Patent landscape for all drugs in clinical trials for a disease (5 hops)

tests.name: Gene to Patent via Full ChEMBL Pipeline (9 hops)
tests.why: Complete gene-to-patent through entire drug discovery chain (9 hops)

tests.name: Drug to Patent Cross-Reference
tests.why: Find patent coverage for a known ChEMBL drug (3 hops)

tests.name: Disease to Protein Targets of Trial Drugs (8 hops)
tests.why: What protein targets are being drugged in clinical trials for a disease

tests.name: Protein Target to All Clinical Trials
tests.why: Reverse: from protein target discover clinical trials for approved drugs (4 hops)

tests.name: Disease to Literature via Trial Drugs
tests.why: Find PubMed articles about drugs in clinical trials for a disease (5 hops)

tests.name: Orphanet to Reactome Pathways
tests.why: Map rare disease to biological pathways via protein (4 hops)

tests.name: Orphanet to Gene Conservation
tests.why: Identify model organisms for rare disease genes (3 hops)

tests.name: Orphanet to Protein Interactions
tests.why: Map rare disease protein interactome (4 hops)

tests.name: Orphanet to Tissue Expression
tests.why: Where rare disease genes are expressed (4 hops)

tests.name: Orphanet to Gene Sets
tests.why: Link rare diseases to MSigDB gene sets (3 hops)

tests.name: OMIM to Toxicogenomic Genes
tests.why: OMIM disease to environmental modifier genes via CTD (3 hops)

tests.name: OMIM to Curated Genes via MONDO/GenCC
tests.why: Multi-authority gene curation from OMIM (4 hops)

tests.name: HPO Phenotype to Drug Candidates via Rare Disease (9 hops)
tests.why: Phenotype-driven drug discovery via rare disease genes (9 hops)

tests.name: Cell Type to Diseases via scRNA-seq
tests.why: What diseases are studied at single-cell level for a cell type

tests.name: Cell Type to Pathogenic Variants
tests.why: Pathogenic variants in cell-type-expressed genes (4 hops with filter)

tests.name: Disease to Cell Types via CellXGene (reverse)
tests.why: Which cell types are captured in disease scRNA-seq datasets

tests.name: Tissue to Diseases from scRNA-seq (reverse)
tests.why: What diseases have scRNA-seq datasets for a tissue

tests.name: Tissue to Cell Types from scRNA-seq (reverse)
tests.why: Complete cellular atlas from tissue scRNA-seq studies

tests.name: Tissue to Drug Targets via Expression (6 hops)
tests.why: Tissue-specific drug target discovery through expression data

tests.name: Disease to Tissue Expression of Causal Genes
tests.why: Where are disease-causing genes expressed (5 hops)

tests.name: Drug to GO Biological Processes (5 hops)
tests.why: From drug compound to biological processes via targets and pathways

tests.name: Drug to Disease via Toxicogenomics (4 hops)
tests.why: Map drug safety profile to disease ontology

tests.name: Drug to Target AlphaFold Structures (4 hops)
tests.why: Get predicted structures for all drug targets

tests.name: Drug Binding Proteins to Gene Names (5 hops)
tests.why: From drug through binding affinity data to gene nomenclature

tests.name: Phenotype to Patents (11 hops!)
tests.why: Longest working chain: clinical phenotype to patent filings (11 hops)

tests.name: Disease Interactome to Drugs (7 hops)
tests.why: Network pharmacology: drugs for disease protein interaction partners

tests.name: Cell Type Interactome Pathways (7 hops)
tests.why: Neuron-expressed protein interaction partner pathways

tests.name: Protein Domain to Gene Names
tests.why: Map protein domain family to human genes (4 hops)

tests.name: Protein Domain to Approved Drugs
tests.why: Find approved drugs targeting a domain family (3 hops)

tests.name: Protein Domain to Pathways
tests.why: Pathway landscape of entire domain family (3 hops)

tests.name: Protein Domain to AlphaFold Structures
tests.why: Predicted structures for domain family (3 hops)

tests.name: Gene Set to Reactome Pathways (5 hops)
tests.why: Map gene set members to biochemical pathways

tests.name: Gene Set to Approved Drugs (5 hops)
tests.why: FDA-approved drugs targeting gene set proteins

tests.name: Gene Set to Pathogenic Variants (4 hops filtered)
tests.why: Pathogenic mutations within a pathway gene set

tests.name: Gene Set Interactome Expansion (6 hops)
tests.why: Expand gene set to full protein interactome (guilt by association)

tests.name: Disease to Gene Sets (reverse, 4 hops)
tests.why: What MSigDB gene sets contain disease-causing genes

tests.name: Disease to Hallmark Gene Sets (filtered)
tests.why: Core biological processes disrupted by a disease (Hallmark only)

tests.name: GO Kinase to Drug Molecules (7 hops filtered)
tests.why: From molecular function to drug chemistry via reviewed proteins (7 hops)

tests.name: GO Lipid Metabolism to SwissLipids
tests.why: Connect biological processes to lipid databases (3 hops)

tests.name: Protein to Disease via Toxicogenomics
tests.why: Safety pharmacology: diseases associated with protein-targeting compounds (5 hops)

tests.name: Disease to PharmGKB Pharmacogenes via GenCC
tests.why: Which validated disease genes are pharmacogenes (4 hops)

tests.name: Drug to Pathways via Pharmacogenes (6 hops)
tests.why: Drug through pharmacogene biology to pathways

tests.name: Phenotype to Drug Candidates via Metabolomics (4 hops)
tests.why: Metabolomics-driven drug repurposing from clinical phenotype

tests.name: Metabolite to Enzyme Reactions
tests.why: Find enzyme reactions involving a metabolite (3 hops)

tests.name: Metabolite to Reactome Pathways
tests.why: Map metabolite to biological pathways via protein targets (3 hops)

tests.name: Metabolite to Drug Targets (7 hops)
tests.why: Full metabolite-to-drug pipeline through ChEMBL (7 hops)

tests.name: Metabolite to Rare Diseases
tests.why: Connect metabolites to rare disease databases via phenotypes (3 hops)

tests.name: Brain Lipid Proteins
tests.why: Find proteins in brain lipid metabolism (3 hops)

tests.name: ChEBI to Protein Interactions
tests.why: Find proteins interacting with a small molecule (3 hops)

tests.name: BioGRID Interaction Partners to Pathways (6 hops)
tests.why: Find pathways shared by protein interaction partners - novel network biology query

tests.name: Metabolite to Rare Disease Genes (4 hops)
tests.why: From metabolite abnormality to Orphanet genes via HPO phenotypes

tests.name: Cell Type to BioGRID Interactions (5 hops)
tests.why: Protein interaction network for cell-type markers

tests.name: Tissue to Lipid Metabolism Pathways (4 hops)
tests.why: Link tissue expression to lipid biology via SwissLipids

tests.name: Cell Type to Approved Drugs (5 hops)
tests.why: Find approved drugs targeting cell-type-specific proteins

tests.name: Phenotype to Protein Interaction Network (5 hops)
tests.why: Interaction partners of phenotype-associated disease genes

tests.name: Pathway to Diseases via ClinVar (4 hops)
tests.why: Diseases linked to pathway genes through clinical variants

tests.name: Phenotype to Clinical Trials (4 hops)
tests.why: Active trials for phenotype-associated conditions

tests.name: Orphanet Disease to ChEMBL Drug Candidates (8 hops)
tests.why: Complete rare disease to drug discovery pipeline

tests.name: Orphanet to PubChem via Phenotypes (5 hops)
tests.why: Compounds for rare disease via HPO/OMIM/CTD path

tests.name: Cell Type to Clinical Trials via CellXGene (4 hops)
tests.why: Find clinical trials for diseases studied in single-cell data

tests.name: Cell Type to SCXA Gene Experiments (CL Bidirectional)
tests.why: NEW: Cell Ontology to gene experiment entries with cell-type-labeled expression (Enhancement 1). Naive B cells have ~75 marker gene entries.

tests.name: Classical Monocyte to SCXA Gene Experiments
tests.why: Classical monocytes (CD14++) have marker gene expression in E-CURD experiments

tests.name: Gene to SCXA Gene Experiment Details
tests.why: Navigate from gene summary to detailed gene-experiment entries with cluster-level expression and cell type labels

tests.name: CD19 Single-Cell Expression
tests.why: CD19 (B cell marker) shows cell-type-labeled expression in E-CURD experiments: naive B cell 70.48 TPM, memory B cell 117.78 TPM

tests.name: CD3E T-cell Single-Cell Expression
tests.why: CD3E (T cell marker) shows expression across T cell subtypes with cell type labels

tests.name: Cell Type to CellxGene Cell Type Data
tests.why: CL to cellxgene_celltype for tissue distribution and disease associations

tests.name: Tissue to CellxGene Cell Types
tests.why: Find cell types present in specific tissues via CellxGene

tests.name: Disease to CellxGene Cell Types
tests.why: Find cell types associated with diseases in CellxGene

tests.name: CellxGene Dataset to Cell Type Details
tests.why: Navigate from dataset to detailed cell type info via CL
